0.text: TCD 001/2024 Architeriors Interior Design (L.L.C) V Emirates National Investment Co (L.L.C)

1.text: DECEMBER 30, 2025 TECHNOLOGY AND CONSTRUCTION DIVISION - ORDERS

3.text: IN THE COURTS OF DUBAI INTERNATIONAL FINANCIAL CENTRE

11.text: ORDER WITH REASONS OF H.E. CHIEF JUSTICE WAYNE MARTIN

12.text: UPON the Judgment of H.E. Justice Roger Stewart dated 31 July 2025 (the “Judgment”)

13.text: AND UPON the Order with Reasons of H.E. Justice Roger Stewart dated 22 August 2025 (the “22 August 2025”)

14.text: AND UPON the Order with Reasons of H.E. Justice Roger Stewart dated 16 October 2025 refusing the Defendant’s Appeal Notice dated 2 September 2025 seeking permission to appeal the Judgment

15.text: AND UPON the Defendant’s Renewed Permission to Appeal Application dated 6 November 2025 seeking to appeal the Judgment and the 22 August 2025 Order (the “Renewed Application”)

16.text: AND UPON the Claimant’s submission in opposition dated 27 November 2025

19.text: 2. The Defendant is ordered to pay the Claimant’s costs of the Renewed Application to be assessed on the standard basis unless agreed.

20.text: 3. Within fourteen (14) days of the date of this Order, the Claimant shall fi le a statement of its costs with respect to the Renewed Application, together with any short submissions in support of the...

21.text: 4. Within fourteen (14) days of service of the Claimant’s statement of costs, the Defendant shall serve any submissions in opposition to the quantum claimed.

22.text: 5. The amount of the Claimant’s costs shall thereafter be assessed by H.E. Chief Justice Wayne Martin on the papers by a process of immediate assessment.

39.text: “44.117 The Court of Appeal will allow an appeal from the decision of the Court of First Instance where the decision of the lower Court was:

40.text:
(1) Wrong; or   
(2) Unjust because of a serious procedural or other irregularity in the proceedings in the lower   
Court.”

41.text: 10. RDC 44.5 requires that an appellant obtain permission to appeal to the Court of Appeal except where the appeal is against a committal order.

43.text: “44.19 Permission to appeal may only be given where the lower Court or the Appeal Court considers that:

44.text: (1) The appeal would have a real prospect of success; or (2) There is some other compelling reason why the appeal should be heard.”

45.text: 12. RDC 44.19 provides that permission to appeal may only be given where the appeal would have a real prospect of success or there is some other compelling reason why the appeal should be heard.

46.text: 13. In the context of an assessment of the prospects of success “real” means realistic rather than fanciful and involves the same test as is applied in applications for immediate judgment.3

47.text: 14. A real prospect of success does not mean a probability of success, but more than mere arguability. 4

48.text: 15. “Some other compelling reason why the appeal should be heard” may include the public interest in clarifying the meaning and scope of relevant practice and provisions of DIFC and wider UAE law.5

49.text: 16. It is established that “real” in the context of an assessment of the prospects of success means realistic rather than fanciful, applying the same test as is applied in an application for immediate...

50.text: 17. It is also established that a real prospect of success does not mean a probability of success, but more than mere arguability.7

51.text: 18. Accordingly, in order to obtain the grant of permission, a prospective appellant needs to establish more than the proposition that the proposed appeal is reasonably arguable – rather, it must be e...

52.text: 19. Particular principles apply to applications for permission to appeal against case management decisions and multi factorial assessments undertaken by a Judge at fi rst instance, given the hurdles w...

53.text: 20. When a renewed application made to the Court of Appeal is refused, it is appropriate for the Court to provide reasons which adequately explain the reasons why permission has been refused. The adeq...

54.text: 21. On the other hand, when a renewed application for permission to appeal is granted, the reasons for the grant can and should be expressed more briefl y, and any views expressed should be expressed ...

59.text: 23. These considerations have led to the adoption of the principle that in order to succeed in a challenge to a Judge’s fi nding of fact, the Appellant must demonstrate that the Judge was plainly wron...
